review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they relate to the review and update of baseline configurations, which is a key aspect of maintaining security posture. The original names were slightly ambiguous regarding the frequency or nature of the review. Changing 'scheduled' to 'periodic' clarifies that these reviews should occur regularly, aligning with best practices for maintaining security baselines.
naming_improvements: Use 'periodic' instead of 'scheduled' to better convey the regularity of the review process.
title: Baseline Configuration - Review and update the baseline...
step1_reasoning: The NIST 800-53 CM-2-b control focuses on the review and update of baseline configurations. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that key security configurations such as RBAC roles, NetworkPolicies, Pod Security Standards, and API server configurations are regularly reviewed and updated. These elements form the baseline security posture of a Kubernetes cluster, similar to how baseline configurations are managed in cloud environments.
